one_line_ask: Take three flat archival live-performance photos and drive all three to a matched noir two-tone (amber-highlight / deep-ink-shadow) duotone treatment with halftone texture and film grain, then deliver each as a print-ready poster-ratio image and a square social crop plus a packaged set and a review board.
brief: Umbra Loft, a fictional basement live-jazz and spoken-word venue, is announcing its autumn "Midnight Sessions" concert series and wants a cohesive poster look built entirely from its own archive. They are handing off three raw archival performance photos pulled from past nights: a stand-up double-bass player under a single stage spot, a vocalist leaning into a vintage ribbon mic, and a wide shot of the trio mid-set on the low stage. As-shot the photos are unusable for a series: each is flat and dull, shot on a phone in near-dark with mixed warm house light and a cool spill from an EXIT sign, slightly tilted, with a cluttered background of cabling, monitors and audience heads. The venue's whole identity is dark, moody and cinematic, and they want the three photos driven to one signature NOIR TWO-TONE (duotone) treatment so they read instantly as a matched set at poster size on the venue wall and at Instagram-thumbnail size in the feed: deep ink-blue shadows lifting to a warm amber highlight, a subtle print-screen halftone texture so it feels like a screened gig poster, fine film grain so the flat inks don't band on large print, and darkened edges so the eye is pulled to the performe
